entry.title: Re: How to match Intercompany Stock Transfer , COGS & Rev...
entry.content:
Hi @SagarSurve,

If you want to match document items within same entity (e.g. the sending company of stock transfer), set up a data source with same field as both Leading Unit and Partner Unit and a matching methods with rules that can identify the matching items (such as the ones you proposed G/L accounts and billing type). For more info, see https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/4ebf1502064b406c964b0911adfb3f01/e44f0ad0da5e4cbc81dc6afcc8e135ca.html?ai=true&version=2025.001.
Combining both intercompany sales and intercompany stock transfer P&L into one matching method is fine, as long as the segregation can be done on matching rule level. The reconciliation report is more dependent on Reconciliation Cases/Display Groups.
P&L with BS accounts are not to be mixed up in one data source/matching method, though, as they have different period setup, i.e. cross-year, carryover, etc. See also question 2.2 in https://me.sap.com/notes/2815304.

Let me know if it answers your question. Thanks!

Grace

entry.title: Re: Withholding Tax Not Recognized in SD Customer Invoices
entry.content:
Hi Marlene,

Thank you for your question. Withholding tax in the Sales Process is supported in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, but requires specific configurations to be in place. Please refer to the SAP Help Portal documentation Setting Up Withholding Tax in the Sales Process for detailed guidance.

This topic explains how to configure and check withholding tax in the sales process. Before you begin, you must first have enabled extended withholding tax for your respective company code and defined withholding tax types and codes for each respective country/region for which you are using extended withholding tax. You'll then need to make a series of configuration settings and maintain specific master data as described in the following sections.

Configuring withholding tax for your sales processes involves a series of configuration steps followed by some discrete changes to specific master data. Once these configurations are made and the master data updated, you can run your sales processes normally and verify that the withholding tax is being calculated and posted at various points.

If you have completed all the configuration steps and withholding tax is still not being calculated, please refer to KBA 3383828 - Withholding tax not calculated - SAP for Me - Scenario 12 for further troubleshooting. If the issue persists, please raise a support case in component FI-AP-AP-Q-2CL and our experts will assist you further.

Best regards,
Gordon
SAP Support

entry.title: How to cancel the specific item in the 3PL inbound delivery?
entry.content:
Hi,

We have an interface to a third-party warehouse provider (SAP_COM_0440). A purchase order and the corresponding inbound delivery were transmitted to this warehouse via the interface.

During the goods receipt process, one item was underdelivered, while another item was overdelivered. For the overdelivered item, a separate inbound delivery was created in the purchase order for the excess quantity and then posted. This inbound delivery has been completed successfully. 

As a result, we are now facing the following issue:

  • We cannot complete the purchase order because the inbound delivery has not been fully received.
  • We cannot delete the inbound delivery because this function is not supported by the interface configuration.
  • Since the inbound delivery already exists, we are also unable to modify the purchase order.

Could you please assist us by removing the open line items from the inbound delivery?

We would appreciate your support in resolving this issue.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to cancel the specific item in the 3PL inbound de...
entry.content:
Hi,

Please Note, in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, if the delivery has been distributed to 3PL Warehouse System, the delivery cannot be deleted.

However, you can achieve the equivalent of a deletion operation by performing a zero-out operation to set the delivery item quantity to zero and complete Goods Receipt operation. After completing these operations, the stocks in the delivery are released.

After checking the inbound delivery 180013161 in your system, I noticed you have reversed the Goods Receipt for it. So,

  • If you only want to remove the specific item from the inbound delivery  >>  You can kindly use the SOAP API Warehouse Stock Transfer Receipt Advice – Receive from Warehouse (B2B) to complete Goods Receipt with updating ActualDeliveryQuantity of the specific item to 0. 
  • If you want to cancel the inbound delivery  >>  You can kindly use the SOAP API Warehouse Stock Transfer Receipt Advice – Receive from Warehouse (B2B) to complete Goods Receipt with updating ActualDeliveryQuantity of each item to 0.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP CI Mapping Output - JSON payload output segme...
entry.content: JSON property order mismatch is expected in SAP CPI because JSON objects are inherently unordered. CPI serialization does not preserve schema sequence. If strict property order is required for external API compliance, build the JSON manually using a Groovy script with to enforce insertion order. Otherwise, rely on consumer flexibility since most APIs ignore property order.

entry.title: Re: Material Interchangeability During Goods Receipt
entry.content:
Hello Antonio, 

To fix this layout issue in transaction MM01, run through the troubleshooting steps below: 

 
1. Missing Global MPN Activation
Even if you configured your material type HERB, the Inventory-Managed MPN sub-screen requires a explicit global switch activation in the system: 
  1. Go to transaction SPRO.
  2. Navigate to: Logistics - General  Material Master  Basic Settings  Make Global SETTING 
  3. Ensure that the Manufacturer Part Number (MPN) functionality checkbox is completely selected/active 
2. Missing Sub-Screen Configuration in OMT3
If the field selection group layout does not pull the sub-screen into the Basic Data screen sequence, it will stay hidden:
  1. Run transaction code OMT3 (or go to Material Master 
     
     Configuring the Material Master 
     
     Define Structure of Data Screens for Each Screen Sequence).
     
  2. Select your standard or custom active Screen Sequence (e.g., for Industry standard).
     
  3. Drill down into Data Screens and choose the Basic Data 1 view.
     
  4. Go to the Sub-screens layout table and check if sub-screen SAPLMGMM with screen number 2016 (or the corresponding MPN data container number) is assigned to a sequential block. If it is missing, you must manually add a line entry for this sub-screen profile.
     
 
3. Field Selection Group Hide Rules (OMS9)
If the block is mapped structurally but hidden dynamically via properties, check the field visibility assignment:
 
  1. Run transaction code OMS9.
  2. Find the field selection group associated with fields MARA-MFRPN (Manufacturer Part Number) and MARA-MFRNR (External Manufacturer).
  3. Locate the Field Selection Group assigned to the material type HERB or your active Industry Sector (C - Chemical industry).
  4. Ensure the radio dial indicator is checked for Optional Entry or Display, rather than marked as Hide. 

waiting your kindly feedback 

Regards,

entry.title: Re: Allocation based on planned hours – S/4HANA Cloud Pub...
entry.content:
Follow below steps : 

  1. Define the allocation cycle in the Manage Allocations app
  2. Set the tracing factor: planned hours from WBS
  3. Map cost center → WBS receiver rules,Confirm that activity type configuration supports the tracing factor; note that enabling activity type as a receiver tracing factor may require a configuration entry in table T811FLAGS (field UIWKGI = 'X') if it is not selectable by default
  4. Execute periodic actual cost allocation via Run Allocations app
  5. Analyze results using the Allocation Flow and Allocation Results apps, or reporting apps such as Project Financial Booklet – Professional Services or Monitor Projects

Useful SAP Learning journey - https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/performing-overhead-cost-controlling

would there still be a need for BTP shadow users to be created (on IAS user authentication) once SAP products have migrated from XSUAA to this AMS?

eg. currently SAP Build Process Automation relies on Role Collection. we have assigned the Role Collection to CIS/IAS user group and are not granting the role collections to BTP Users directly. However it is still required for the shadow user to exist in the BTP subaccount (with empty role collection, as these same users are assigned to the user groups already in IAS) otherwise there is an error page "BTP shadow user not found for user provided by external identity provider"

entry.title: @sap/cds was loaded from different locations
entry.content:
Environment

  • npm: 11.12.1
  • Node.js: 24.13.1
  • @SAP/cds-dk (global): 10.0.5

Hello,

I'm running into a very strange issue with a newly created CAP project, and I'm hoping someone can shed some light on what's happening.

The project is basically a bare CAP application. If it only contains the backend service, everything works fine. However, as soon as I add a generated Fiori application, the server fails to start with the following error:

$ npm start

> nai-rtr-budget-mgmt@1.0.0 start
> cds serve

[cds-plugin-ui5] [INFO] Running cds-plugin-ui5@0.13.6 (@sap/cds-dk@9.9.3, @SAP/cds@9.9.3)
[cds-plugin-ui5] [INFO] Mounting /budgetadmin to UI5 app C:\Dev\nai-rtr-budget-mgmt\app\budget-admin (id=budget-admin)
[cds] [INFO] loaded model from 5 file(s):
 
  srv\index.cds
  app\services.cds
  app\budget-admin\annotations.cds
  srv\budget-service.cds
  db\budget.cds

[cds] [INFO] connect to db > sqlite { url: 'db.sqlite' }
[cds] [INFO] using auth strategy {
  kind: 'mocked',
  impl: 'node_modules\\@sap\\cds\\lib\\srv\\middlewares\\auth\\basic-auth.js'
}
[cds] [INFO] serving BudgetService {
  at: [ '/odata/v4/budget' ],
  decl: 'srv\\budget-service.cds:3',
  impl: 'node_modules\\@sap\\cds\\srv\\app-service.js'
}
info fiori-tools-proxy Using UI5 version 1.150.0 based on manifest.json
info fiori-tools-proxy Starting fiori-tools-proxy using following configuration:
info fiori-tools-proxy proxy: 'undefined'
info fiori-tools-proxy ignoreCertErrors: 'false'
info fiori-tools-proxy backend: undefined
info fiori-tools-proxy ui5: [{"path":"/resources","url":"https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com","version":"1.150.0"},{"path":"/test-resources","url":"https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com","version":"1.150.0"}]
info fiori-tools-proxy debug: 'false'
info fiori-tools-appreload Livereload middleware started for port 35729 and path C:\Dev\nai-rtr-budget-mgmt\app\budget-admin\webapp
(node:29036) [DEP0190] DeprecationWarning: Passing args to a child process with shell option true can lead to security vulnerabilities, as the arguments are not escaped, only concatenated.
(Use `node --trace-deprecation ...` to show where the warning was created)
[cds] [INFO] loaded model from 5 file(s):
 
  srv\index.cds
  app\services.cds
  app\budget-admin\annotations.cds
  srv\budget-service.cds
  db\budget.cds

[cds] [INFO] loaded model from 5 file(s):
 
  srv\index.cds
  app\services.cds
  app\budget-admin\annotations.cds
  srv\budget-service.cds
  db\budget.cds

info fiori-tools-preview Initialized for app budgetadmin
[cds-plugin-ui5] [ERROR] Failed to inject application pages to welcome page! The links of the application pages may not work properly!

-----------------------------------------------------------------------
ERROR: @SAP/cds was loaded from different locations:
 
  c:\dev\nai-rtr-budget-mgmt\node_modules\@sap\cds
  ~\appdata\roaming\npm\node_modules\@sap\cds-dk\node_modules\@sap\cds 

Ensure a single install to avoid hard-to-resolve errors.
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

I can work around the issue by uninstalling the globally installed @SAP/cds-dk, but that doesn't seem like a real solution.

What's confusing is that I have another CAP project on the same machine that also uses cds-plugin-ui5, and it works perfectly regardless of whether @SAP/cds-dk is installed globally or not. I also noticed that project even has @SAP/cds-dk installed locally as a dev dependency, yet it still runs without any issues.

Here are the things I've already tried:

  • Matched the package versions between the working and non-working projects.
  • Downgraded the globally installed @SAP/cds-dk from v10 to v9 (since the working project was originally created with v9).
  • Installed @SAP/cds-dk locally instead of relying on the global installation.
  • Verified that the issue only occurs after adding the Fiori application. A backend-only CAP project runs fine.

None of the above made any difference. The new project continued to fail while the existing project continued to work.

The only things I've found that make the new project work are:

  1. Completely removing any additional @SAP/cds-dk installation so only a single installation is available.

Strangely, adding a .env file containing: DEBUG=all also allows the project to start, even with the global @SAP/cds-dk installed. I don't understand why enabling debug logging would affect this behavior.

Has anyone encountered something similar before? More importantly, why would two CAP projects on the same machine behave differently when they appear to have the same tooling and dependency versions? Is there anything specific I should compare between the two projects to determine why one resolves @SAP/cds correctly while the other does not?

Any suggestions would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Error AFABN
entry.content:
Solution Checklist

1. Verify AO90 Account Determination: - Check all G/L accounts maintained for Depreciation Area 08. Ensure no mandatory account is missing (Asset Balance Sheet, Accumulated Depreciation, Depreciation Expense

2. Check Reconciliation Account Settings: - If Area 08 is an inflation/posted periodically or depreciation-only area, the assigned G/L accounts must not be Fixed Asset Reconciliation Accounts. As per SAP guidance (Note 7595), reconciliation accounts are generally only appropriate for the main area posted in real time (typically Area)

3. Verify Depreciation Area Customizing (OADB/OAYZ): - Confirm Area 08 is correctly defined as an inflation area. Check whether the posting indicator (real-time/periodic/not posted) is configured correctly.

4. Check Account Assignment

  1. Ensure all required accounts exist for
  2. Company Code G235
  3. Chart of Accounts
  4. Asset Class
  5. Account Determination

5. Run Consistency Checks

  1. Execute AW01N and verify APC values and planned depreciation.
  2. Check ANLB/ANLC data consistency if required.

6. Review SAP Notes

  1. 3490950 – AAPO191 "Asset row is not complete.
  2. 2073134 – Errors AAPO510/AAPO191 during AFAB
  3. 859617 – AAPO191 in depreciation posting (referenced by SAP Communi

Since you have already verified:

  1.  AO90 configuration
  2. Asset Master (AS03)
  3. Asset Values (AW01N)
  4. Planned depreciation
  5. SAP Notes

The next thing to verify is whether the G/L accounts assigned to Depreciation Area 08 are incorrectly marked as Asset Reconciliation Accounts in FS00. This is the most common root cause of AAPO191 for inflation or periodically posted depreciation areas.

If you can share: S/4HANA or ECC version

  1. Screenshot of AO90 for Area 08
  2. Full AAPO191 error log (SLG1/AFABN log)

I can help identify the exact configuration issue.

entry.title: Re: Issue in order settlement thorugh KO88
entry.content:
The settlement through KO88 cannot be executed because it is linked to an asset in FY2025, which is already closed.

To complete the settlement, FY2025 must be temporarily reopened, the settlement executed, and then the fiscal year closed again.

entry.title: Re: No item category exists (Table T184L ZUMB NORM CHSP)...
entry.content: Hi @Beto1: Whenever batch determination takes place, the system uses the combination of Delivery Type (ZUMB) + Item Category Group (NORM) + Item Usage (CHSP). Please verify whether the batch-related settings have been maintained. If they are maintained, the system will determine Item Usage = CHSP automatically. For this same combination (ZUMB + NORM + CHSP), you also need to maintain the corresponding Item Category Determination settings so that the correct item category is determined during delivery.

I faced exactly the same problem yesterday; I had a quick look at it and managed to solve it using the following variable: CDS_PLUGIN_UI5_ACTIVE=false

It’s not the best solution, because you always have to rebuild the apps, but it works.

entry.title: Re: How can we bring all three currencies — LC, FC, and G...
entry.content:
Hi Aagya,

FC means Functional currency, right?

in ACDOCU there are 3 currency key figures. TC-Transaction Currency, LC-Local Currency and GC-Group Currency.

TC will be transferred from ACDOCA, when the FS item has a breakdown by TC in its BDC.

If you fill target key figure GC from e.g.  global currency source key figure, then you cannot transfer both currencies , LC and FC in parallel because you have only LC as target key figure.

BR

entry.title: How to directly add returnable packaging items in Outbound Delivery without VL316 error?
entry.content:
Hi experts,
We are working with Scope Item BDW (Returnable Packaging Processing) and facing an issue when adding a returnable packaging material directly as a line item in an Outbound Delivery (VL02N / Manage Outbound Deliveries).

[Issue]
When adding the item directly to the Outbound Delivery, we get the following error:
Error Message: VL316 - Sales area xxxx 00 00 is not permitted.
Context: The material master uses a common distribution channel (00). When added directly in the delivery, the system tries to determine the sales area using channel 00, which is not set up for this sales org/plant.

[Observed Behavior]
If we add the returnable packaging item in the Sales Order (SO), it works fine because the line item inherits the header distribution channel (e.g., 10).

[Question]
Is there any workaround to allow adding line items directly in the Outbound Delivery using the header distribution channel (or bypassing the common channel 00 determination)?
Any insights or recommendations on how to handle this requirement directly within the delivery document would be greatly appreciated!

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Currency Conversion in SAP Datasphere: Understanding TCUR* Tables and Practical Implementation
entry.content:
Hi

Congratulations with the blog. The topic has been touched by many others and I don't see anything new, sorry. If you want to use AI, fine but use it to do something new and not seen before.

You write "Financial reports", it is my experience that it is very difficult to mimic the currency conversion happening in financial reports and you should in general avoid it. The problem most often comes down to being able to find the correct reference date, it is for sure not the posting date but rather the "Exchange Rate Date". In finance the currency conversion is already handled using more measures in different currencies.

with kind regards

Kristian

entry.title: Compressing (Activating) a DataMart ADSO dont behave as expected
entry.content:
We have a datamart ADSO that is getting additive deltas. The key figures are all set to summation, yet when we activate a data load to compress the data to reduce the space it takes up, it seems it is overwriting data and not summing the data.

I compared two identical ADSO's, one setup as standard and the other is the datamart. They were identical until I compressed the first load. After compression the values were not the same anymore. The two ADSO's now differ with 37 million ZAR, but we only expected a record count drop and not a value difference.

RECORDMODE is not mapped - as per Joule this is the expected outcome and the correct setting:

RECORDMODE Value

Meaning

Impact on Data Mart ADSO

' ' (blank)

After Image — the status after a change or addition

The standard additive record. Correct for Data Mart ADSO when the source provides additive deltas. If all records arrive as after images (e.g., because RECORDMODE is not mapped), the ADSO will SUM all of them — which may produce inco

entry.title: Re: Contingent worker in Compensation Module SSF
entry.content: Thanks for pointing me to the "Check Valid EC Users" tool in Provisioning - I checked our Contingent Worker there and the result is: Valid EC User = Yes, with a full EmpJobInfoEO record (effective from 2024-09-02 to 9999-12-31). So she is a fully valid EC user, not a non-EC/UDF case. This means the Hybrid template angle doesn't apply here, since Hybrid is meant to bring in non-EC users - she's already recognized as EC. To recap what we've ruled out at this point: her Employee Status is Active, with the same raw picklist code as a Regular Employee who passed the same Add Employees check, and now she's also confirmed as a Valid EC User. Despite all of this, she's still rejected with "Selected user is not eligible for compensation" when added to a Compensation worksheet.

entry.title: SAP MDK FioriToolbar OnPress events are triggered for the wrong button
entry.content:
I'm facing an issue with the SAP MDK FioriToolbar using the latest MDK version.

Scenario

I have a page with three FioriToolbar buttons:

  • Confirm
  • Next
  • Previous

    Each button has its own OnPress rule.

    ConfirmItem

    • Title: Confirm
    • OnPress: $(PLT,'/Actions/NavigateToConfirm.action','/Actions/NavigateToConfirm.action','/Rules/NavigateToConfirmWeb.js')

      NextItem

      • Title: Next
      • OnPress: /Rules/CreatePONextButton.js

        PreviousItem

        • Title: Previous

OnPress: /Rules/CreatePOPreviousButton.js

Issue

When I click the Previous button, the Confirm button's OnPress rule (NavigateToConfirmWeb.js) is also executed.

I verified this by adding console.log() statements to each rule.

The output is:

CreatePOPreviousButton
NavigateToConfirmWeb

The Previous rule does not call NavigateToConfirmWeb, so it appears that the framework is triggering the Confirm button's OnPress handler.

 

Additional observations

If I remove the Confirm button from the toolbar, the Previous button works correctly.

The issue occurs only when all three toolbar buttons are present.

The Previous rule does not navigate to the Confirm page.

The Confirm button is the only one using a $(PLT,...) expression, while the other buttons directly reference JavaScript rules.

I am testing using the latest MDK Web client.

Has anyone experienced this issue before?

Is this a known issue with FioriToolbar on the MDK Web client?

Is mixing $(PLT,...) and JavaScript rules in the same toolbar supported?

Are there any recommended workarounds?

Thanks in advance for any suggestions

entry.title: Re: France Statutory Payment Issue
entry.content:
#GenAI-Assisted Content

Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. 

I asked our internal chatbot for support, and here's the response. Please check whether the suggestions help you resolve your issue:

Custom Logic for Populating <Ustrd> Tag

To meet the business requirement of populating the <Ustrd> tag based on the document type, you can implement custom logic using SAP S/4HANA Cloud’s extensibility features. Here is a step-by-step guide:

  1. Create Custom Logic:

    • Open the Fiori app ‘Custom Fields and Logic’.
    • Click the ‘+’ button to create a new Custom Logic. 3066300 - How to bring the Header Text of supplier invoice to the Text field of Input Tax G/L account in FI document.
    • Set the Business Context to ‘Accounting: Journal Entry’ and choose the BAdI ‘Journal Entry Item Substitution’.
    • Implement the logic to check the document type and populate the <Ustrd> tag accordingly.

  2. Implement Logic:

    • Use the following example code as a reference:
      DATA lv_ustrd TYPE string.
IF accountingdocheader-accountingdocumenttype = 'YA'.
  lv_ustrd = accountingdocitem-sgtxt.
ELSE.
  lv_ustrd = accountingdocitem-xblnr.
ENDIF.
accountingdocitemout-ustrd = lv_ustrd.
substitutiondone = abap_true.
    • This code checks if the document type is ‘YA’. If true, it assigns the value from the SGTXT field to <Ustrd>. Otherwise, it uses the XBLNR field.

  3. Publish the Logic:

    • Save and publish the custom logic to activate it in your system.

By following these steps, you can ensure that the <Ustrd> tag is populated correctly based on the document type, fulfilling your business requirement.

For more details, see the following information:

[1] 3066300 - How to bring the Header Text of supplier invoice to the Text field of Input Tax G/L account in FI document.

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: Statutory Payment Remittance issue - Document type YA
entry.content:
#GenAI-Assisted Content

Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. 

I asked our internal chatbot for support, and here's the response. Please check whether the suggestions help you resolve your issue:

Custom Logic for DMEE Tree in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

To address the requirement of populating the SGTXT field information under the tag based on the ‘YA’ document type, and using the ‘XBLNR’ field for other document types, you can implement custom logic using SAP S/4HANA Cloud’s extensibility features. Here is a step-by-step guide:

  1. Create Custom Fields: Use the ‘Custom Fields and Logic’ app Custom logic for Payment Files - SAP Community to create any necessary custom fields that might be required for your logic.

  2. Implement Custom Logic:

    • Navigate to the ‘Custom Logic’ app.
    • Create a new enhancement implementation SAP S/4HANA Cloud: Configuring Custom Field in Decision Table Output using the appropriate business context and BAdI.
    • Write ABAP code to check the document type and populate the tag accordingly. For example:
      IF document_type = 'YA'.
  tag_value = SGTXT.
ELSE.
  tag_value = XBLNR.
ENDIF.

  3. Update DMEE Tree:

    • Access the DMEE tree configuration.
    • Use the custom logic to map the fields in the DMEE tree structure.
    • Ensure that the logic is correctly integrated to populate the tag based on the document type.

  4. Testing:

    • Test the implementation thoroughly to ensure that the correct values are being populated based on the document type.

By following these steps, you can customize the DMEE tree to meet your specific business requirements in SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

For more details, see the following information:

[1] Custom logic for Payment Files - SAP Community
[2] SAP S/4HANA Cloud: Configuring Custom Field in Decision Table Output

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: Flexible WF: Deadline Notification - Email to Manager...
entry.content:
#GenAI-Assisted Content

Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. 

I asked our internal chatbot for support, and here's the response. Please check whether the suggestions help you resolve your issue:

Understanding the ‘Manager of Last Approver’ in Flexible Workflow

In SAP’s Flexible Workflow, the ‘Manager of Last Approver’ option is typically used to determine the manager of the person who last approved a work item. However, as you correctly pointed out, if the work item has not yet been approved, there is no ‘Last Approver’ to reference. This means that the system cannot populate the ‘LastApprover’ field until an approval action has been completed.

Why ‘LastApprover’ is Empty

Your understanding is correct: the ‘LastApprover’ field is populated only after an approval action has been completed. If the work item is still pending approval, the workflow container (AC_CONTAINER) will not have a value for ‘LastApprover’, resulting in the error message ‘No recipients found’.

Possible Solutions

  1. Alternative Escalation Logic: Consider using a different logic for escalation, such as sending an email to the manager of the current approver if the work item remains unapproved for a certain period.

  2. Custom BAdI Implementation: You might need to implement a custom BAdI to handle escalation based on the time elapsed without approval, rather than relying on the ‘Last Approver’.

  3. Workflow Configuration: Ensure that your workflow configuration includes conditions that can trigger an escalation email based on the time elapsed, rather than the approval status.

Conclusion

The ‘Manager of Last Approver’ Using the agent determination - Manager of Approver option is not suitable for escalation purposes when the work item has not yet been approved. You will need to explore alternative methods to achieve your requirement.

For more details, see the following information:

[1] Using the agent determination - Manager of Approver

Flexible Workflow

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: How to create Business Partner Master Data to S4Hana...
entry.content:
#GenAI-Assisted Content

Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. 

I asked our internal chatbot for support, and here's the response. Please check whether the suggestions help you resolve your issue:

Steps to Create/Replicate Business Partner Master Data in S/4HANA Public Cloud

Based on the steps you’ve completed, here is a guide to ensure the replication of Business Partner Master Data from SAP Master Data Governance (MDG) to S/4HANA Public Cloud:

  1. Verify Communication Arrangement: Create Communication Arrangement for Business Partner Replication Ensure that the communication arrangement in S/4HANA Public Cloud is correctly set up with scenario ID SAP_COM_0008. This arrangement should facilitate the integration between MDG and S/4HANA Cloud.

  2. Check Destination Configuration: Confirm that the destination in SAP BTP is correctly configured with the inbound delivery service URL and that the authentication details are accurate.

  3. Approval Process: The approval process for business partner creation typically involves the ‘Manage Inbound Processes’ app in S/4HANA Cloud. This app allows you to organize and track the decentral creation and change processes for business partner data.

  4. Monitor Inbound Processes: Use the ‘Manage Inbound Processes’ app to check if the business partner data replication process is initiated and awaiting approval. This app should show the status of the replication and any pending approvals.

  5. Check My Inbox: If the process is not visible in the MDG tenant’s ‘My Inbox’, ensure that the workflow configuration is correctly set up to route approval tasks to the appropriate inbox.

  6. Additional Configuration: Ensure that the MDG Inbound Process Configuration section in the communication arrangement is set with the correct process templates for creating and changing business partners.

If these steps are correctly followed and configured, the business partner data should replicate successfully from MDG to S/4HANA Public Cloud.

For more details, see the following information:

[1] Create Communication Arrangement for Business Partner Replication
[2] Manage Inbound Processes - Business Partners

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: PBXX condition type via custom logic during purchase...

Hi AmanB,

You can avoid usage of the last PO price by setting the user parameter EVO.

IMG: MM -> Purchasing -> Environment data -> Default values for Buyers-> Setting for Default values.
Select or setup a default value and on the "price adoption" tab you may choose how copying of conditions from last purchase order must behave. Select "Do not copy".

Regarding the custom logic to allow overwriting the condition price if the manual price is higher, you should implement a custom check, I cannot help here - maybe someone else has an idea.

Best regards,

Timea

entry.content:

SSCUI: 101518 - Define Automatic Credit Control

BD6 

In our tenant, the entries under credit control area 1000 are delivered with
Currency = USD and Fiscal Year Variant = K4 for every risk category:

Credit Control Area | Risk Category | Credit Group | Description | Currency | Update | FYV
1000 | A | 01 | Orders w/ default no risk | USD | 000012 | K4
1000 | B | 01 | Orders w/ default low risk | USD | 000012 | K4
1000 | C | 01 | Orders w/ default medium risk | USD | 000012 | K4
1000 | D | 01 | Orders w/ default high risk | USD | 000012 | K4
1000 | E | 01 | Orders w/ default highest risk | USD | 000012 | K4

Two questions:

1. Currency
Is it possible to set a currency other than USD (in our case JPY)?
If yes, is it maintained in this SSCUI, or in a different configuration
activity (e.g. credit segment / credit control area definition)?
If it is not changeable, what is the impact of leaving it as USD when the
company code currency is JPY?

2. Fiscal Year Variant
Is the Fiscal Year Variant configurable here?

which object it is derived from.

Any pointer to the relevant SAP Help Portal documentation would be appreciated.

entry.title: Re: France Statutory Payment Issue
entry.content:
Hi @Palaksha_SA,

Short answer: There is no delivered switch that fills the Ustrd tag from SGTXT vs XBLNR by document type, and the document type is not exposed as a source field in the payment mapping, so you build this with the payment-medium BAdI FBL_EVENT_06 feeding a custom reference field that you then map to Ustrd.

Where the Ustrd tag sits

Ustrd is the unstructured remittance element of the ISO 20022 pain.001 credit transfer (path RmtInf → Ustrd, one occurrence, max 140 characters). For France, the delivered credit-transfer formats are FR_CGI_XML_CT (pain.001.001.03) and FR_CGI_XML_CT_V9 (pain.001.001.09) — see Country/Region-Specific Payment Format Mappings.

Can you do it purely in Map Format Data, without ABAP?

No — not for this rule. In Map Format Data for Payments an element can be driven by conditional atoms, and a Condition can compare a Structure Field with a Constant using operators such as Equal to (Node Types, Condition). But in the delivered FR_CGI_XML_CT mapping the document type is not available as a source Structure Field — only item fields such as FPAYP-SGTXT and FPAYP-XBLNR are. Since the driver of the condition is missing, the rule cannot be built with format-tree conditions alone.

Recommended solution — BAdI FBL_EVENT_06 → custom reference field → Ustrd

As key-user extensibility. Use the BAdI Payment Medium: Custom Additional Reference Fields (FBL_EVENT_06) to fill a custom reference field, then map that field to the Ustrd node. You can include up to 10 fields, 132 characters each (Including Custom Reference Fields, KBA 3511049).

On granularity: the BAdI exposes the changing parameter PAYMENTPAIDITEMS (table of paid items), which lets you set ZREF01–ZREF05 at paid-item level — exactly what you need to evaluate the document type per document (Including Custom Reference Fields).

Steps:

  1. Custom Logic app → new implementation of FBL_EVENT_06, add filter FORMAT = your format.
  2. In the code, loop PAYMENTPAIDITEMS; per item read the document type (it is not in the paid-items structure, so read it from a released CDS view via the item's document keys) and set ZREF01 to SGTXT when document type = YA, otherwise XBLNR — both fields are available on the paid item.
  3. In Map Format Data for Payments, map ZREF01 to the Ustrd node (Map Format Data for Payments).

Alternative split: put only the document type (or a YA flag) into ZREF01 in the BAdI, and in the format create two atoms under Ustrd — condition ZREF01 = 'YA' → source FPAYP-SGTXT, otherwise → FPAYP-XBLNR. Same result, field selection stays declarative.

Constraints to check

Existing condition: the Ustrd node already carries a condition (structured vs unstructured remittance, e.g. FPM_CGI-STRD = SPACE) — keep your atoms inside it so you don't break Strd/Ustrd exclusivity.
Length: Ustrd max 140 characters, ZREF max 132.
Grouping: SGTXT is line-item text — decide the handling when several items are grouped into one payment.

Summary

No standard toggle exists and no modification is needed. Fill a custom reference field with BAdI FBL_EVENT_06 using the paid-items parameter (ZREF01–ZREF05, item level): document type = YA → SGTXT, otherwise XBLNR, then map that field to Ustrd in Map Format Data for Payments (in your own format copy). Mind the 140-character Ustrd limit and the existing structured/unstructured condition on the node.

Hope it helps.
Best regards,
Michal

entry.title: Re: Email Notification for Credit-Blocked Sales Order Wit...
entry.content:
Some of the standard alternatives:

1) Situation Handling (custom type)
Instead of reusing FIN_DCD_APPROVAL_2, you can create a custom Situation Type based on the UKM_DCD object (Documented Credit Decision). Define the trigger condition: DCD created with status = 10 (New). Configure the action to send an informational email to the credit director (via team assignment). This avoids the approval dependency and simply reacts to the creation of a blocked DCD.

2) Business Event → Communication Arrangement
When a sales order is credit blocked, the system raises a business event (e.g., CreditCheckBlocked). You can subscribe to this event using Event Mesh or Integration Suite and route it to an email service (Outlook, SMTP, or SAP Build Process Automation). This is a standard extensibility option and doesn’t require 1QM.

3) Output Management (BRF+)
Technically, you could configure an output determination rule for the sales order credit block scenario, but this is less elegant and usually reserved for customer documents. Still, it’s possible to generate an “internal notification” output type that emails the credit director.

entry.title: SAP Fiori tools and Fiori elements at UI5con 2026 with building blocks, runtime AI, automated tests
entry.content:
UI5con 2026 took place on 14 July in St. Leon-Rot, Germany — the annual community conference for SAPUI5 developers. This year our team had a strong presence with five sessions: a slot in the opening keynote, a dedicated talk on what's new in SAP Fiori Elements V4 and Runtime AI, a hands-on workshop on flexible development with Fiori Elements, a joint session on the redesigned FLP Sandbox 2.0, and a deep dive into metadata-driven test generation. We focused on the latest innovations, including the SAP Fiori MCP server and Claude Code plug-in, embedded runtime AI features built into the SAP Fiori apps that SAP ships with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, automated testing tools, a hands-on workshop featuring building blocks, and a completely redesigned local development sandbox. Here's a summary of everything we shared.

Keynote: Agentic Capabilities for SAP Fiori

 

Klaus Keller speaking about new agentic capabilities during the keynote.

 

 

Klaus and Ian had a 7-minute slot in the opening keynote to present the latest agentic capabilities in SAP Fiori — covering the MCP Server, the growing library of developer skills, and the SAP Fiori Claude Code Plugin that brings it all together, specifically:

The SAP Fiori MCP Server

The MCP Server for SAP Fiori gives AI agents full awareness of your Fiori stack. It can:

  • Search Fiori, UI5, and annotation documentation
  • Scan your project for existing Fiori apps
  • Fetch OData service metadata directly from your SAP system
  • Generate a new Fiori app for a CAP or RAP/OData V4 service
  • Add, change, or remove UI elements through a guided workflow

It runs where you already work: pre-installed in SAP Business Application Studio (Fiori and Full Stack dev spaces), and auto-available via the SAP Fiori tools extension pack in Visual Studio Code.

The SAP Fiori Claude Code Plugin

Building on the MCP server, the SAP Fiori Claude Code Plugin is the all-in-one package for Claude Code users — MCP server plus all skills bundled, nothing to wire up manually. The Skills available today cover the most common development tasks.

Klaus then gave a live demo, installing the plugin and using the skill to add an analytical column chart to a List Report page in an existing travel app — showing the full flow from a natural language instruction to a rendered chart in the app.

Session: What's New — SAP Fiori elements V4, Runtime AI & My Home

Presented by Klaus Keller and Ian Quigley, slides are available here

Ian Quigley sharing what’s new in SAP Fiori elements for OData V4.

 

Our main talk covered two areas: the latest Fiori elements framework capabilities and the expanding set of runtime AI features.

SAP Fiori elements in 2026

SAP Fiori elements is the UI development framework that generates SAP Fiori apps at runtime based on OData services and annotations — and this year has been significant for it. Nearly 450,000 apps have been created in the first half of the year, underlining just how central the framework has become.

The flexibility model has also matured. Developers can now choose their starting point and combine approaches freely:

  1. Floorplans — standardised layouts for enterprise consistency
  2. Custom Pages with Building Blocks — mix SAP Fiori elements building blocks into your own page structure
  3. Freestyle SAPUI5 Pages — maximum flexibility, with full access to building blocks alongside custom code

This flexibility extends to extension points within floorplans as well, giving teams a clean spectrum from fully generated to fully custom.

Runtime AI Features

The talk introduced the growing set of SAP Fiori AI features shipping as part of SAP S/4HANA Cloud:

  • Smart Summarization — generally available since release 2502
  • Error Explanation — generally available since release 2502
  • Easy Filter — generally available since release 2502, with Sorting & Grouping and Variant Management support new in 2608
  • Easy Fill — new in release 2608
  • Smart Helpers — introduced as the next evolution of in-app AI assistance

Coming soon: these features are also planned for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition (2025 FPS02).

Getting started is straightforward — see SAP note 3651017 for enablement steps. Base AI features are available at no additional cost.

Workshop: Flexibility at Scale with Freestyle SAPUI5 and SAP Fiori elements

Presented by Christoph Gollmick

The team on stage answering questions during the hands-on workshop.

 

 

This 2-hour hands-on workshop demonstrated how to build flexible, enterprise-grade user interfaces by combining freestyle SAPUI5 and SAP Fiori elements technologies. Participants worked with a CAP backend based on the SFLIGHT sample service to construct a travel dashboard featuring an object-centric design — a practical, end-to-end scenario that showcased the full flexibility spectrum in action.

The session was aimed at beginners and gave attendees direct, guided experience moving across the three development approaches: using Fiori elements floorplans for consistent, generated UI; enriching pages with Building Blocks; and stepping into freestyle SAPUI5 where the app demands it. Working in SAP Business Application Studio (or VS Code with SAP Fiori tools), participants built real app screens against a real backend — not just slides.

Session: Metadata-Driven Test Generation for SAP Fiori elements V4

Presented by Dominik Heim

 

Dominik Heim talking about automated testing. There were a lot of mentions of cats

 

 

Writing comprehensive OPA5 integration tests for SAP Fiori elements apps has historically been a manual, time-consuming process — standard generators typically produce only a "hello world" scaffold and leave the rest to you.

Dominik's session introduced a new approach that changes this entirely. By combining the SAP Fiori elements test library with the OData V4 service metadata that already describes your app, SAP Fiori tools can now automatically generate OPA5 tests that cover the specific features enabled by your metadata out of the box. The initial heavy lifting is handled for you; developers can then focus their effort on testing unique business logic and custom extensions rather than writing boilerplate.

The key benefits Dominik walked through:

  • Higher coverage from day one — metadata-driven generation means tests reflect what your app actually does, not just a generic scaffold
  • Maintained flexibility — the generated tests are a starting point you can layer manual enhancements on top of, not a black box
  • Dramatically reduced overhead — moving from empty test files to a verified, running application in a fraction of the usual time

Session: Honey, I Shrunk the Boilerplate — FLP Sandbox 2.0

Presented by Johannes Gluch (SAP UI5 Core) and Dominik Heim

Testing SAP Fiori applications locally has always meant wrestling with the FLP Sandbox — a solution that has grown organically over the years, bringing complex SAP-internal configuration requirements, copy-pasted boilerplate, and sparse documentation with it. This made it the right moment for a complete redesign.

Johannes opened the session by presenting the new FLP Sandbox 2.0 — a modern, stable, and minimal-setup replacement built on a declarative configuration approach. A single boot manifest file replaces the scattered boilerplate of the past. The session included a live migration demo, walking through the transformation of an existing application from the legacy sandbox to the new approach.

Dominik then showed how SAP Fiori tools completes the picture on the tooling side. The key integration: Fiori tools can serve the new boot manifest as a virtual endpoint, meaning the only change needed in your project's ui5.yaml is a single configuration option to opt in to the new sandbox — no file to maintain manually.

The session also previewed enhancedHomePage, a new myHome-based FLP experience for local development that surfaces everything in one place:

  • All available virtual endpoints — similar to the CAP landing page on cds watch
  • Terminal warnings inline — no more scrolling through terminal output to find issues
  • A preview of generated cards stored with your app — filling a previous gap where the card generator produced cards with no way to preview them

Both the Fiori tools sandbox integration and the enhancedHomePage are currently lab previews, but stay tuned.

A Great Community Event

UI5con is always a highlight of the year — a chance to connect with the wider SAPUI5 community, hear what other teams are building, and get direct feedback from developers in the room. This year's edition reinforced just how much momentum there is around AI-assisted development with UI5, and it was genuinely exciting to show what the Fiori Claude Code Plugin can already do today.

Check out the session recordings on the UI5 YouTube channel.

If you have any questions about the event or want to discuss your SAP Fiori elements projects with us, please join our monthly developer roundtable or comment below.

entry.title: How are you handling the reconciliation of opening subcontracting stock under Indian GST in SAP?
entry.content:
I’m curious to know what is Standard or Best approach Opening Subcontracting Stock Reconciliation using transaction J1IGRECON.

The Scenario:

Imagine you have migrated opening subcontracting stock (e.g., 100,000 kg of raw material) sent out to vendor locations across multiple, different historical subcontracting challans:

  • Migration: Stock is uploaded using movement type 561 O (Opening balance for stock provided to vendor).

  • Goods Receipt: The finished goods/processed materials are subsequently received back via movement type 101 (with 543 O for raw material consumption).

The Technical Dilemma:

Since the stock was initialized via 561 O without a direct reference to original pre-migration 180-day GST tracking challans in the system:

  1. How do you map and reconcile the 101 Goods Receipts against the uploaded opening challans in J1IGRECON?

  2. How are you handling line-item level matching when bulk quantity (100,000 kg) is distributed across dozens of legacy challans?

  3. What best practices or custom workarounds have worked best for you to prevent GST compliance issues (like 180-day ITC reversal/tax liability risks)?

entry.title: Re: PBXX condition type via custom logic during purchase...
entry.content:
Hello @AmanB 

First of all, I would not use PO item processing BAPI - that's way too powerful for the task.

Then try Condition Exclusion. The blog post Condition Exclusion Functionality provides details on the configuration. Although both SAP Help page and the blog post above point to SD, condition exclusions work the same in MM-PUR.

If that doesn't work, you could bring both condition types, i.e. the manual one and the one determined by a condition record. Then you could use either condition requirement or condition value formula (defined in VOFM transaction) to implement your condition comparison logic.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: SAP Business One – Cross Business Partner Down Payment Reconciliation (Same Vendor, Different Branch
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am facing a challenging scenario in SAP Business One and would appreciate your guidance.

Scenario:

             1. Vendor Bangalore – CardCode: XXX1

             2. Vendor Chennai – CardCode: XXX2

Both Business Partners represent the same legal vendor but are maintained as separate Vendor Master records because they operate from different locations.

Our company made an advance/down payment of ₹8,412,000 to Vendor Bangalore (XXX1).

Later, the vendor requested that this advance be adjusted against pending AP Invoices belonging to Vendor Chennai (XXX2).

Issue:

SAP Business One does not allow reconciliation between two different Vendor Business Partners. Since the down payment belongs to XXX1 and the AP invoices belong to XXX2, I am unable to adjust or reconcile the amount.

I have already explored several approaches, including:

  • Internal Reconciliation
  • Payment Cancellation and Recreation
  • Journal Entry adjustments
  • Down Payment reconciliation scenarios

However, none of these methods allow settlement across different Vendor CardCodes without causing accounting issues.

Questions:

  1. Is there any standard SAP Business One functionality to adjust a Vendor Down Payment from one Business Partner to another?
  2. What is the recommended accounting treatment in this scenario?
  3. Has anyone handled a similar requirement in a live implementation?
  4. Are there any SAP Notes or best practices that address this situation?

Any guidance or real project experience would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Message No. F0379 When F110S

When you have some issue and create a discussion in this forum, please try to share as much information as possible with related screen shots.  This will help the members to assist you in right direction.  

Coming to your issue, check SAP Note 3030943

entry.title: Re: OKB9 ERROT -An entry already exists with the same key
entry.content:
How OKB9 works
OKB9 maintains default account assignments (profit center, cost center, business area, etc.) for combinations of company code + account + controlling area. The “key” is not just profit center and cost center — it includes controlling area, company code, and account assignment object. The error means, some combination of these fields already exists, even if you don’t see it with your current selection.

Why you don’t see it in TKA3P
TKA3P stores the entries, but depending on your filters (controlling area, chart of accounts, company code), you may not be searching with the exact key. Sometimes the duplicate is hidden because:

  • A different validity period exists (date ranges overlap).
  • The entry exists with a blank or default profit center/cost center.
  • The entry is tied to a different company code or account assignment object than the one you’re checking.

entry.title: Re: Building and Deploying Your First MCP Server on SAP BTP
entry.content:
@Willem_Pardaens Actually, at this moment I don't have much to say about SAP's golden AI architecture. The MCP gateway on CPI needs enablement of the Edge integration cell, which needs a Kubernetes cluster on-premises (SAP ECS, we use S/4HANA private cloud) and all that comes with it, operations and costs.   Why I need all this hassle just to talk to our S/4HANA APIs. 

The only problem I have with hosting the MCP on BTP, is the end-to-end SSO with principal propagation, which doesn't work because of how the current Joule studio on SAP Build interacts with CF hosted applications, it's like SAP BPA application call to the CF application (no way to pass the user context).  Today I can't build an enterprise custom Joule agent for my business users to let them use Joule for audit sensitive operations. 

Not sure why SAP can't solve this problem first and let us run the MCP on BTP CF at least for talking to their own applications which are not SAAS.  Waiting for Joule studio 2.0, will this still be the case that we need MCP Gateway/Edge integration cell?

Thanks

Amit

entry.title: Re: Problem with discarding entity using CAP as a proxy o...
entry.content:
@aravindhan2529 I have tried using an Fiori Elements App directly consuming the RAP Service and the draft / discard works as expected.

I compared the Callstacks and found the following:
FE on RAP does:

GET QuotationHeader(QuotationUUID=cb113f97-ff18-1fe1-9edb-827015d7ceef,IsActiveEntity=false)$selectDraftAdministrativeData … 

POST (QuotationUUID=cb113f97-ff18-1fe1-9edb-827015d7ceef,IsActiveEntity=false)(…).Discard

GET(QuotationUUID=cb113f97-ff18-1fe1-9edb-827015d7ceef,IsActiveEntity=true)/_Items…

GET (QuotationUUID=cb113f97-ff18-1fe1-9edb-827015d7ceef,IsActiveEntity=true)….

 On the other hand our FE on CAP Service does:

GET (QuotationUUID=cb113f97-ff18-1fe1-9edb-827015d7ceef,IsActiveEntity=false)$selectDraftAdministrativeData…

POST(QuotationUUID=cb113f97-ff18-1fe1-9edb-827015d7ceef,IsActiveEntity=false)(…).Discard

GET (QuotationUUID=cb113f97-ff18-1fe1-9edb-827015d7ceef,IsActiveEntity=false)/_Items…


BATCH 
	GET (QuotationUUID=cb113f97-ff18-1fe1-9edb-827015d7ceef,IsActiveEntity=false)/SiblingEntity?$select=isActiveEntity,QuotationUUID
	
	GET(QuotationUUID=cb113f97-ff18-1fe1-9edb-827015d7ceef,IsActiveEntity=false)…
ENDBATCH

Our service "officially" fails on the first part of the batch request. It seems to loose the SiblingEntity navigationproperty in the proxy-request.  Though that would probably not help, since it would then return an empty result, same as we have with the /_Items call.

entry.title: Re: creating and opening connection for SAC
entry.content:
Dear @emarsonA ,

A connection in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) is created to establish secure communication between SAC and the required data source, such as SAP S/4HANA, SAP BW, SAP HANA, SAP Datasphere, or another supported system. To create a connection, navigate to the Connections section in SAC, select the appropriate connection type, and provide the required system details, including the server information, authentication method, and user credentials. Once the information is entered, the connection should be tested to verify successful communication before saving it. After the connection has been created, it can be opened and used when creating models or stories in SAC. Users simply select the required connection from the list of available connections and access the relevant data source to import data or establish a live connection, depending on the project requirements. This enables secure and efficient access to enterprise data for reporting, analysis, planning, and dashboard development within SAP Analytics Cloud.

entry.title: How to Add Additional Currency Fields to the Cost Centers Review Booklet
entry.content:
In this blog, we want to illustrate how to use data source extensions in the Custom Fields (F1481) app to add additional currency fields such as functional and freely defined currencies to the Cost Centers Review Booklet (F7763). The standard settings in the Cost Centers Review Booklet expose the global and company code currency. 

Understanding the Data Source Architecture 

Before diving in, it’s important to understand the underlying data model. The Cost Centers Review Booklet is built on CDS analytical queries that sit on top of a shared cube: 

Layer 

CDS View Name

Used For 

Query 

Cost Center Actuals - Query (C_COSTCENTERACTUALQUERY) 

Actuals pages 

Query 

Cost Center Plan and Actual Values - Query (C_COSTCENTERPLANACTUALQUERY) 

Plan/Actuals pages 

Cube 

Cost Center Review Booklet - Cube (I_ACTPLNCOSTCENTERRPTGCUBE) 

Underlying cube for both queries 

This two-layer architecture is critical because it determines where you need to create your data source extension: 

If the field is available in the CDS query but simply not visible in the Cost Centers Review Booklet, you only need to create a data source extension directly on the query.  

If the field is not available in the CDS query but exists in the underlying cube, you need to create a data source extension on the cube, publish it, then create a second one on the query. 

Key Rule: For cube-based analytical queries, always extend the cube first, then the query. 

Additional currency fields such as functional and freely defined currency are available in the CDS query. Therefore, we will focus on data source extensions on the query in this blog. 

Step-by-Step Procedure 

Step 1: Verify the Required Currency Fields Are Not Yet Available 

Before making changes, confirm that the currencies you want to add are not yet available in the Cost Centers Review Booklet. 

  1. Open the Cost Centers Review Booklet app. 
  2. Set your filter parameters (for example, company code 1010) and choose Go.
  3. Open the Layout section in the side panel and check the available currencies. 

 

 

Step 2: Create a Data Source Extension on the CDS Query 

  1. Open the Custom Fields app. 
  2. Switch to the Data Source Extensions tab.
  3. Choose Create. 
  4. Select the Data Source Cost Center Actuals – Query (C_COSTCENTERACTUALQUERY) if you want to add the dimension on Actuals pages or Cost Center Plan and Actual Values - Query (C_COSTCENTERPLANACTUALQUERY) if you want to add the dimension on Plan/Actuals pages. 
  5. Enter an Extension ID (for example, YY1_CURRENCYREVIEWBOOKLET) and a meaningful description. Then, choose Create.
  6. In the Available Fields panel, search for the currencies you need.
  7. Choose Save, then Publish. 

Tip: Use the field search (magnifying glass icon) to quickly look up data source fields. You can switch between technical and human-readable names using the toggle in the upper right corner. 

 

Step 3: Display the Added Currencies in the Cost Centers Review Booklet 

Now, verify that the currencies appear in the app: 

  1. Open the Cost Centers Review Booklet app. 
  2. Set your filter parameters and choose Go. 
  3. Open the Layout section in the side panel and check for the added currencies in the Available Objects list under Measures.

The additional currency fields now appear in the report and can be used for analysis. 

 

Further Reading 

Creating Data Source Extensions 

Cost Centers Review Booklet 

Center Actuals - Query (C_COSTCENTERACTUALQUERY) 

Cost Center Plan and Actual Values - Query (C_COSTCENTERPLANACTUALQUERY) 

Cost Center Review Booklet - Cube (I_ACTPLNCOSTCENTERRPTGCUBE) 

 

Do you have questions or feedback? Feel free to leave a comment below or contact us on the SAP Community!

entry.title: Re: Building and Deploying Your First MCP Server on SAP BTP
entry.content: Hi @AmitBangotra , that is valid feedback that you share. But in order to use MCPs in Integration Suite there is no need for Edge Integration Cell. The new API Centric integrations via Integration Cell make that possible in the cloud. (see https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/api-centric-integration-in-sap-integration-suite-a-new-paradigm-for-api-amp/ba-p/14438245 and https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/mcp-gateway-in-sap-integration-suite-your-apis-ready-for-the-age-of-agents/ba-p/14438250). This allows you to enable MCPs on top of SAP APIs without any custom development. Beyond the Basic/OAuth authentication in the current release, additional SSO concepts (SAML and principal propagation) are on the roadmap to be used for MCP consumption.
